title: Eurostat: EU household gas prices up in the second half of 2025
content: In the second half of 2025, household gas prices in the EU continued to show the seasonal fluctuation between semesters that was visible before the 2022 crisis, with this pattern distorted during 2022 and 2023.
snippet: In the second half of 2025, household gas prices in the EU continued to show the seasonal fluctuation between semesters that was visible before the 2022 crisis, with this pattern distorted during 2022

title: EU launches a call to boost strategic investments and further deepens its partnership with Armenia
content: Today, at the first EU-Armenia Summit, the European Commission launched a Call for Expressions of Interest aimed at identifying concrete investment opportunities across key sectors in Armenia – such as transport, energy, and digital – and accelerating their implementation.

title: EU and Armenia sign connectivity partnership, strengthen economic ties and deepen security cooperation
content: Today, the European Union and Armenia held their first ever Summit in Yerevan, reinforcing cooperation in the areas of connectivity, security and defence, economic development and people-to-people contacts.

title: Forthcoming publication of the annual surplus indicator (total number of allowances in circulation) for the Market Stability Reserve under the EU Emissions Trading System
content: On 29 May 2026 at 18:00 CET, the European Commission will publish the total number of allowances in circulation (TNAC) on the EU carbon market in 2025.
snippet: On 29 May 2026 at 18:00 CET, the European Commission will publish the total number of allowances in circulation (TNAC) on the EU carbon market in 2025.

title: Report shows Europe’s fish and seafood sector shaped by crisis
content: A new report by the European Market Observatory for fisheries and aquaculture products (EUMOFA) shows how a series of crises - the COVID-19 pandemic, Brexit, and Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine - have reshaped Europe’s fish and seafood sector.

title: Commission publishes simplification review of EU Deforestation Regulation
content: The report on the simplification of the revised EUDR and a set of further measures aim to make the law's implementation more effective.
snippet: The report on the simplification of the revised EUDR and a set of further measures aim to make the law's implementation more effective.

title: Boosting knowledge circulation: new study on economic impact of possible measures
content: A new study analyses the economic impact of possible measures to improve access to and reuse of research results.
snippet: A new study analyses the economic impact of possible measures to improve access to and reuse of research results.

title: Spain hosted the seventeenth EQAVET peer review of the 2024-26 cycle on ‘Self-assessment tools for EQAVET descriptors in the context of VET providers’
content: The peer review was organised by the Spanish EQAVET National Reference Point (NRP) at the Ministry of Education, Vocational Training and Sports, in close cooperation with the Department of Education, Vocational Training, Physical Activity and Sport, Government of the Canary Islands
snippet: The peer review was organised by the Spanish EQAVET National Reference Point (NRP) at the Ministry of Education, Vocational Training and Sports, in close cooperation with the Department of Education,

title: EU and Armenia deepen their relations in a first Summit in Yerevan
content: The first-ever EU-Armenia Summit will take place on 4-5 May, in Yerevan. This marks a significant step forward in their relations, reaffirming the European Union's strong commitment to Armenia's sovereignty, resilience and reform agenda.

title: Commission assesses with EU countries the security of oil and gas supply as the conflict in the Middle East continues
content: Today’s Oil Coordination Group (OCG) and Energy Union Task Force Security (EUTF Security) meetings gathered experts from the European Commission, industry, EU countries, the International Energy Agency (IEA), and NATO to discuss the gas and oil security of supply situation in Europe.
snippet: Today’s Oil Coordination Group (OCG) and Energy Union Task Force Security (EUTF Security) meetings gathered experts from the European Commission, industry, EU countries, the International Energy Agenc

title: Commission acts to support EU sugar producers amid market pressures
content: EU countries approved a Commission proposal to temporarily suspend the inward processing for imported cane sugar
snippet: EU countries approved a Commission proposal to temporarily suspend the inward processing for imported cane sugar

title: Commission proposes €2 million to help over 800 dismissed workers in Belgium
content: The European Commission has proposed €2 million in funding to help 803 workers who lost their jobs after the bankruptcy of Belgian car glass manufacturer Soliver. The money will come from the European Globalisation Adjustment Fund for Displaced Workers (EGF).
snippet: The European Commission has proposed €2 million in funding to help 803 workers who lost their jobs after the bankruptcy of Belgian car glass manufacturer Soliver. The money will come from the European

title: Commission aims to tackle ‘Terrible Ten’ Single Market barriers by publishing guidance for EU countries on proportionality
content: New Commission guidance aims to reduce Single Market barriers by supporting EU countries in applying proportionality and improving compliance with EU notification obligations.
snippet: New Commission guidance aims to reduce Single Market barriers by supporting EU countries in applying proportionality and improving compliance with EU notification obligations.

title: Citizens Energy Package: helping households in the energy transition
content: A package of recommendations aimed at protecting and supporting citizens in engaging in the clean energy transition has been presented by the Commission today.
snippet: A package of recommendations aimed at protecting and supporting citizens in engaging in the clean energy transition has been presented by the Commission today.

title: Europol published report on the latest trends in the cybercrime landscape
content: Europol published the new edition of the Internet Organised Crime Threat Assessment (IOCTA) 2026, providing an analysis of the latest trends and developments in cybercrime affecting the EU. The report highlights how cyber threats are growing faster and becoming more advanced and sophisticated.
snippet: Europol published the new edition of the Internet Organised Crime Threat Assessment (IOCTA) 2026, providing an analysis of the latest trends and developments in cybercrime affecting the EU. The report

title: EU funded project POP-ART helps protect public spaces through advanced technology
content: Challenges are growing from threats such as terrorism, malicious use of drones and sudden outbreaks of violence. Police and security teams need smarter tools to spot danger fast and act immediately. The EU funded project POP-ART introduces high-tech solutions on how to spot and manage these threats.

title: Successful first round of the EU Hydrogen Mechanism to support market development, providing value added to the industry
content: The European Commission has successfully closed the first round of activities of the Hydrogen Mechanism operated under the EU Energy and Raw Materials Platform.
snippet: The European Commission has successfully closed the first round of activities of the Hydrogen Mechanism operated under the EU Energy and Raw Materials Platform.

title: Commission adopts temporary State aid framework to support sectors affected by Middle East crisis
content: The European Commission has adopted a temporary State aid framework to enable Member States to support the EU economy in the context of the Middle East crisis
snippet: The European Commission has adopted a temporary State aid framework to enable Member States to support the EU economy in the context of the Middle East crisis

title: European Commission partners with the European Defence Agency on BraveTech EU
content: The European Commission and the European Defence Agency (EDA) have signed a Contribution Agreement to launch the second phase of the BraveTech EU initiative, marking a significant step forward in EU-supported defence innovation.
snippet: The European Commission and the European Defence Agency (EDA) have signed a Contribution Agreement to launch the second phase of the BraveTech EU initiative, marking a significant step forward in EU-s

title: Eurostat: EU household electricity prices stable in 2025
content: In the second half of 2025, average electricity prices for households in the EU remained largely stable, with a slight increase to €28.96 from €28.79 per 100 kWh in the first half of the year. These price levels remain well above levels from before the 2022 energy crisis.

title: Member States move ahead with Commission proposal to reinforce data sharing in fight against cross-border tax fraud
content: The Council of the EU has agreed a position on a proposed amendment strengthening cooperation and VAT data sharing between national tax authorities, the European Public Prosecutor's Office (EPPO), the European Anti-Fraud Office (OLAF), and the Eurofisc network of anti-fraud experts.
snippet: The Council of the EU has agreed a position on a proposed amendment strengthening cooperation and VAT data sharing between national tax authorities, the European Public Prosecutor's Office (EPPO), the

title: EU-Moldova High-Level Energy Dialogue: Renewed commitment to closer energy cooperation
content: The European Commission and the Republic of Moldova held today the 7th High-Level Energy Dialogue in Brussels, reaffirming their strong partnership on energy security and Moldova’s gradual integration into the European energy market.
snippet: The European Commission and the Republic of Moldova held today the 7th High-Level Energy Dialogue in Brussels, reaffirming their strong partnership on energy security and Moldova’s gradual integration

title: Commission acts against unfairly traded imports of adipic acid
content: Today the Commission imposed anti-dumping duties on imports into the EU of adipic acid from the People’s Republic of China.
snippet: Today the Commission imposed anti-dumping duties on imports into the EU of adipic acid from the People’s Republic of China.

title: Commission welcomes Serbia joining SEPA schemes, allowing for faster and cheaper euro transactions for citizens and businesses
content: Today, 18 banks in Serbia have officially joined the Single Euro Payments Area (SEPA) schemes.
snippet: Today, 18 banks in Serbia have officially joined the Single Euro Payments Area (SEPA) schemes.

title: Green Deal Projects Support Office final event: highlighting concrete pathways to accelerate Europe’s green transition
content: At its final event, the Green Deal Projects Support Office showcased results and next steps from EU-funded projects to scale solutions across Europe.
snippet: At its final event, the Green Deal Projects Support Office showcased results and next steps from EU-funded projects to scale solutions across Europe.

title: Commissioner Šuica in Algiers to discuss EU-Algeria cooperation
content: Today and tomorrow, 4-5 May, Commissioner for the Mediterranean Dubravka Šuica is in Algiers, a key strategic partner for the EU, to discuss EU-Algeria bilateral and regional cooperation. title: The Commission welcomes outcome of the IMO 84th Marine Environment Protection Committee
content: The Commission welcomes the outcome of the IMO's 84th MEPC meeting.
snippet: The Commission welcomes the outcome of the IMO's 84th MEPC meeting.

title: Calculation framework for new building life-cycle Global Warming Potential
content: An EU framework for calculating the life-cycle global warming potential (GWP) of new buildings has been published today, aimed at ensuring EU countries use comparable methodologies for this calculation.

title: Seaweed-based textile dyes: a sustainable innovation
content: Textile dyes made from seaweed are being developed by Zeefier, an innovative company based in the Netherlands.
snippet: Textile dyes made from seaweed are being developed by Zeefier, an innovative company based in the Netherlands.

title: EU grants worth €600 million available for cross-border energy infrastructure projects
content: The Commission has today launched a new call for proposals for key cross-border EU energy infrastructure projects with an indicative budget of €600 million.
snippet: The Commission has today launched a new call for proposals for key cross-border EU energy infrastructure projects with an indicative budget of €600 million.

title: EU-Mercosur interim trade agreement starts to provisionally apply
content: Tomorrow (1 May), the EU-Mercosur Interim Trade Agreement (ITA) will start being provisionally applied. This will allow EU producers, exporters, and farmers to start reaping the benefits of this deal as of day one.
snippet: Tomorrow (1 May), the EU-Mercosur Interim Trade Agreement (ITA) will start being provisionally applied. This will allow EU producers, exporters, and farmers to start reaping the benefits of this deal

title: Commission holds high-level talks to shape landmark Circular Economy Act
content: Executive Vice-President Stéphane Séjourné and Commissioner Jessika Roswall led the discussions with key stakeholders.
snippet: Executive Vice-President Stéphane Séjourné and Commissioner Jessika Roswall led the discussions with key stakeholders.

title: Commissioner Brunner visits Serbia to strengthen migration and border management cooperation
content: On 29 April, Commissioner Brunner was in Serbia to further strengthen cooperation on migration, border management and internal security. Serbia plays an important role in managing irregular migration along the Western Balkan route and closely cooperates with EU agencies, including Frontex.
snippet: On 29 April, Commissioner Brunner was in Serbia to further strengthen cooperation on migration, border management and internal security. Serbia plays an important role in managing irregular migration

title: Commission welcomes important step to modernise EU social security coordination rules
content: The European Commission welcomes the approval by EU Member States of the revision of the social security coordination rules. The revised rules are an important step towards fair labour mobility in the EU.

title: New study examines skills and perceptions in the EU defence industry
content: DG DEFIS publishes a Study on the Attractiveness of the Defence Industry, commissioned to the consortium of Novaspace, RAND Europe and Verian. The study collected EU-wide data on how the defence industry is perceived by students, young professionals, ...
